reforms.description: The government aims to tie public R&D funding to private-sector funding and explore ways to enhance public-private interaction, including new business R&D schemes that offer grants and vouchers for R&D projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking public funding to private investment, the reform is expected to stimulate business R&D and enhance collaboration, leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is exploring ways to spur greater public-private interaction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public R&D funding is allocated, aiming to enhance collaboration between public research institutions and the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Tie public R&D funding to private-sector funding, and explore other ways to spur greater public-private interaction.

reforms.description: The National Network of Commercialisation Centres is being established to improve collaboration among public research organisations involved in commercialisation activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration in commercialisation, the initiative is expected to increase the effectiveness of transferring research outcomes to the market, thereby boosting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Network of Commercialisation Centres is being established.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is a significant step towards improving the commercialisation of research, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The National Network of Commercialisation Centres is being established to improve collaboration among those involved in commercialisation activities at public research organisations.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government introduced the Welcome Home Loan initiative to assist low- and moderate-income households in purchasing their first home by providing loans up to NZD 350,000, backed by insurance against loan loss.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates access to home ownership, potentially increasing economic stability and consumer spending among low- to moderate-income households.
reforms.status_evidence: Since its introduction, almost 6,000 households have benefited from Welcome Home Loans.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve access to home ownership for lower-income households, addressing affordability issues in the housing market.
reforms.source_quote: The government provides various programmes to assist low- and moderate-income households to purchase their first home.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government has committed up to NZD 1.5 billion to provide ultra-fast broadband via fibre optic cable, aiming to make it accessible to 75% of New Zealanders within the next 10 years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This investment in broadband infrastructure is expected to enhance connectivity, stimulate economic activity, and support innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed funding and outlined plans for the initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in national infrastructure that is expected to have long-term positive effects on economic growth and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government has committed up to NZD 1.5 billion to provide ultra-fast broadband via fibre optic cable accessible to 75% of New Zealanders within the next 10 years.

reforms.description: The government is considering partial floats of State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs) to improve their performance by subjecting them to corporate governance disciplines and enhancing transparency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving SOE performance through enhanced governance and access to capital can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is currently considering the merits and viability of selling minority stakes in some of the SOEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the governance of SOEs, aiming to enhance their efficiency and performance, which is crucial for the overall economic landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently considering the merits and viability of selling minority stakes in some of the SOEs.

reforms.description: The government has lowered the corporate tax rate from 30% to 28% effective April 2011, aiming to improve the investment climate and attract foreign capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering the corporate tax rate is expected to enhance the attractiveness of New Zealand for investment, thereby potentially increasing business activity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The corporate tax rate was lowered as part of the 2010-11 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aligns New Zealand's corporate tax rate more closely with those of other OECD countries, potentially improving the investment climate.
reforms.source_quote: The corporate tax rate was also lowered from 30% to 28% effective April 2011.

reforms.description: KiwiSaver, a voluntary tax-favoured retirement savings scheme, was introduced to improve private saving incentives, with government matching contributions to encourage participation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing private savings, KiwiSaver aims to increase the overall level of national savings, which can lead to greater investment and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: KiwiSaver was introduced in July 2007 as part of a government strategy to improve private saving incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of KiwiSaver represents a significant policy initiative aimed at enhancing private savings and improving the financial security of New Zealanders.
reforms.source_quote: KiwiSaver is a voluntary tax-favoured defined-contribution retirement savings scheme.

reforms.description: The government has introduced a performance-linked funding system for tertiary education institutions, which will link up to 5% of government funding to the performance of these institutions, aiming to reduce wasted expenditure on low-priority courses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking funding to performance, the reform aims to enhance the quality of education and training, thereby improving the skills of the workforce and supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The performance element has been introduced to the student loans scheme and funding reallocations have occurred.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in funding allocation that aims to improve educational outcomes and workforce skills, which are crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: From 2012, up to 5% of government funding for tertiary education institutions will be performance linked.

reforms.description: The government has reviewed the Industry Training Fund to ensure that funded programmes are of high quality, reallocating under-used funding to support extra university places where there is excess demand.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By ensuring high-quality training programmes and addressing excess demand in universities, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Under-used funding from industry training was reallocated to fund extra places in universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it reallocates resources to meet demand in higher education, thereby enhancing the skill base necessary for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Industry Training Fund has been reviewed to ensure funded programmes are of high quality.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government supported the establishment of the Agricultural GHG Research Centre with 100% government funding to lead research on livestock emissions for the Global Research Alliance on Agricultural Greenhouse Gases.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to develop technologies that mitigate agricultural greenhouse gas emissions, which can enhance productivity and sustainability in the agricultural sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Government supported the establishment of the Agricultural GHG Research Centre with full funding.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Centre represents a significant investment in targeted research to address a critical environmental issue, with potential long-term benefits for the agricultural sector.
reforms.source_quote: In 2010, the Government supported the establishment of the Agricultural GHG Research Centre, with 100% government funding.
